summary: Closed, unmerged no-diff PR used as a match-progress report. It recorded 5 fully matched Kirby ftKb_SpecialN animation functions, 1 pending toy.c function, and 7 failed or partial match attempts across stage, player, UI, and unknown/generated-symbol areas. Because there were no changed files or diff bytes, this should be treated as tracking evidence rather than landed code.

key_files.role: Referenced as the file containing the 5 reported 100% matched Kirby SpecialN animation functions.
Referenced as containing pending function un_80307828.

what_changed: No repository files changed in the captured PR slice; changed_files was 0 and diff_bytes was 0.
The PR body/comment reported 5 matched functions, 7 failed functions, and 1 in-progress function.
Reported matched functions in ftKb_SpecialN.c: ftKb_SpecialNSpit_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirNSpit_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialNDrink_Anim.

Reported failed or partial attempts: grPura_80212FC0 failed 3x; grOnett_801E5194 best 78.1%; pl_80038628 best 55.6%; fn_8021E994 best 97.4%; grCastle_801CE19C best 91.8%; InitNameEntryUIState best 37.3%; grOnett_801E40E4 best 96.1%.
The PR was closed without merge.
smart_moves: The progress report grouped work into matched, pending, and failed sections, making it easy to triage follow-up decomp work.

The matched work was clustered around related Kirby SpecialN animation callbacks, which is useful because nearby fighter action-state routines often share naming, control flow, and data-access patterns.
Failed attempts preserved best observed match percentages, which helps future contributors prioritize near-matches such as fn_8021E994 at 97.4% and grOnett_801E40E4 at 96.1%.
The report kept exact symbol names and file names where known, making later searches straightforward.
decomp_lessons: Do not treat a closed no-diff progress PR as landed code; verify later commits or PRs before relying on these matches.
Progress tracking is still useful when it records exact function names, match percentages, and failed attempts.
Related fighter callback clusters, such as ftKb_SpecialN animation functions, are good batching targets because solved routines may reveal shared naming and structural patterns.

Near-matches with high percentages should be revisited with focused attention on small ordering, float, register-allocation, or type/struct-layout differences, but this PR did not include diff evidence for the specific cause.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter functions used the ftKb_ prefix.
Kirby SpecialN routine names used action-oriented names such as SpecialNSpit, SpecialAirNSpit, SpecialNDrink0, SpecialNDrink1, and SpecialNDrink.
Grounded and aerial variants were distinguished with SpecialN versus SpecialAirN.
Unresolved or address-based symbols retained module/prefix plus address forms such as grPura_80212FC0, grOnett_801E5194, pl_80038628, fn_8021E994, and un_80307828.
InitNameEntryUIState appeared as a descriptive UI-state initializer name rather than an address-only symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use 100% match status as a signal that exact functions may be reusable reference points for adjacent routines.
Retain failed-attempt counts and best percentages to avoid repeating known unsuccessful attempts.
Prioritize high-percentage failures first when looking for quick wins: fn_8021E994 at 97.4%, grOnett_801E40E4 at 96.1%, and grCastle_801CE19C at 91.8%.
The slice provides no assembly diff, source diff, or reviewer discussion, so no concrete code-generation tactic can be confirmed.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the captured slice.
No reviewer feedback or requested code changes can be inferred.
The only human-text evidence was a generated progress report noting match statuses.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs or commits for ftKb_SpecialNSpit_Anim and the other ftKb_SpecialN matched functions to confirm where they actually landed.
Search for un_80307828 in toy.c to see whether the pending function was later matched.
Revisit high-percentage failed functions fn_8021E994, grOnett_801E40E4, and grCastle_801CE19C for potential near-match fixes.
Check whether wip/pending-matches produced another PR with an actual diff.
Look for later renames of address-based symbols listed in this progress report.
